adventure.title: You're Trapped in a Bag!

credits.author: Sam Seer
credits.source: Adapted from a one-page role playing adventure submitted to the 2023 One Page Dungeon Competition (https://www.dungeoncontest.com/opdc-2023)

adventure.introduction: You wake up. The last thing you remember is a night out on the town. Now you seem to be in a giant cave made of... canvas?

atmosphere.setting: A vast, lightless cave whose walls are woven canvas, lit by a faint, sourceless luminescence. Everything is stale, silent, and unsettlingly scaled; the murk below hides heaped piles of discarded oddments.
atmosphere.tone: Pulpy fantasy with a wry, slightly gross sense of humor, underlaid by real claustrophobic menace: something hungry lurks in the dark, and the way out — if there is one — is not obvious. Escape will require careful exploration, deduction, and perhaps a friend.

axe_head.name: Axe Head
axe_head.description: The broad, flat head of a giant battleaxe, wedged high against the wall of an immense cave. The walls are woven canvas, lit by a faint, sourceless luminescence that barely holds back the darkness. Above head height the walls converge into a low dome, scrunched shut in the center. The very tip of the axe blade has torn a small rip in the fabric. From here the axe handle, about the width of a narrow sidewalk, slopes down into the murk.

exits.direction: Clamber carefully down the axe handle

failure.narrative: You stagger under the giant key's monstrous weight and have to set it down before it drags you off your feet. You are not getting anywhere with this thing — not without help.

exits.direction: Drop down into the darkness below

failure.narrative: You teeter at the edge under the giant key's monstrous weight, but cannot bring yourself to drop with it. You set it down with a grunt. You are not getting anywhere with this thing — not without help.

success.narrative: The walls glow with a faint luminescence that has no source — no flames, no glowing moss, no crystals. You deduce that the glow is magical, a side effect of whatever enchantment pervades this place.

failure.narrative: The walls glow faintly, but you cannot tell what causes it.

result.narrative: You drop from the axe head into the darkness and crash down onto the piled rubbish far below, bruising yourself badly.
As you pick yourself out of the rubbish, the dwarf woman scrambles up from under her overturned soup ladle with a clatter of armor, gaping at you in astonishment.

axe_handle_upper.name: Axe Handle (Upper)
axe_handle_upper.description: A stretch of the sloping axe handle, about the width of a narrow sidewalk. Masses of sticky webbing cling to the handle here, dimming the glow from the canvas walls so that your footing is hard to make out. Strange wrapped masses of different sizes are stuck in the webs. The handle continues up toward the axe head, and down toward even denser webbing; over the edge lies unseen darkness.

exits.direction: Clamber back up the axe handle

failure.narrative: You stagger under the giant key's monstrous weight and have to set it down before it drags you off your feet. You are not getting anywhere with this thing — not without help.
You stagger under the giant key's monstrous weight and have to set it down before it drags you off your feet. You are not getting anywhere with this thing — not without help.

exits.direction: Drop down into the unseen darkness below

failure.narrative: You teeter at the edge under the giant key's monstrous weight, but cannot bring yourself to drop with it. You set it down with a grunt. You are not getting anywhere with this thing — not without help.

result.narrative: You drop from the handle into the unseen darkness and slam into the piled rubbish below.

axe_handle_lower.name: Axe Handle (Lower)
axe_handle_lower.description: The lower stretch of the axe handle, choked with dense, heavy webbing that must be forced through to continue along the path. Peering over the side, numerous irregularly-shaped objects are just visible heaped on the cave floor below, and a drop from here looks survivable. The handle continues up toward sparser webbing, and down to the cave floor.

web_cleared.description: The blocking web has been forced through; passage along the handle is thenceforth free.

entered_from.description: Which side the player last entered from: "above" (from axe_handle_upper) or "below" (from bag_floor).

exits.direction: Force your way back up the web-choked handle

success.narrative: You force your way through the clinging mass of webbing, tearing open a passage you can use freely from now on.

failure.narrative: You strain against the dense webbing, but cannot force your way through.

success.narrative: You slice through the clinging webbing with the toenail clipping, tearing open a passage you can use freely from now on.

failure.narrative: You slash at the dense webbing with the toenail clipping, but cannot force your way through.

exits.direction: Force your way down the web-choked handle

success.narrative: You force your way through the clinging mass of webbing, tearing open a passage you can use freely from now on.

failure.narrative: You strain against the dense webbing, but cannot force your way through.

success.narrative: You slice through the clinging webbing with the toenail clipping, tearing open a passage you can use freely from now on.

failure.narrative: You slash at the dense webbing with the toenail clipping, but cannot force your way through.

exits.direction: Drop over the side of the handle

failure.narrative: You teeter at the edge under the giant key's monstrous weight, but cannot bring yourself to drop with it. You set it down with a grunt. You are not getting anywhere with this thing — not without help.

success.narrative: Peering over the side of the handle, you make out a heap of giant empty potion bottles, copper pieces, corks, and moldy sandwiches piled on the cave floor below.

failure.narrative: Peering over the side, you can make out only vague shapes in the gloom below.

success.narrative: From below come faint sounds: the clank of metal and low, miserable muttering. Someone — or something — is down there.
A movement above catches your eye: eight glittering eyes watch you from the dense webs overhead. A huge spider lurks in the shadows.

result.narrative: You drop over the side and land on the piled rubbish below, momentarily winded but unhurt.

bag_floor.name: Bag Floor
bag_floor.description: The floor of the cave, buried under a loose pile of giant-sized rubbish: copper pieces, empty potion bottles, used corks, lint, moldy sandwiches, and more disgusting items. The base of the giant axe rests in the center of the pile, its handle rising into the gloom overhead. A giant overturned soup ladle lies against the pile like a makeshift shelter.
bag_floor.soft_item_guidance: Nondescript giant-sized rubbish the player might pick up or use: copper pieces, empty potion bottles, used corks, lint balls, moldy sandwiches, a bent spoon, a broken belt buckle, a single moldy boot, a tattered glove, a dented tin cup, an apple core, a chipped die, a snapped quill, a length of frayed rope, a wineskin with no cork.

exits.direction: Clamber back up the axe handle

failure.narrative: You stagger under the giant key's monstrous weight and have to set it down before it drags you off your feet. You are not getting anywhere with this thing — not without help.

exits.direction: Squeeze through the flap in the floor

failure.narrative: You try to squeeze through the flap with the giant key, but its monstrous weight defeats you; you set it down with a grunt. You are not getting anywhere with this thing — not without help.

result.narrative: Among the refuse, you notice a giant toenail clipping, its edge curved and sharp. Pried loose, it could be wielded like a sword.

success.narrative: This is not a random pile of junk. Copper pieces, potion bottles, rations — these are some adventurer's supplies, much like your own missing pack.

failure.narrative: It's a disgusting heap of giant rubbish, nothing more.

success.narrative: An adventurer's supplies, giant-sized yet all shrunken to fit — you realize you are inside a magical Bag of Holding. The rubbish's proportions are uneven: the bag's magic shrinks items by different amounts to make them fit.

failure.narrative: The heap of giant supplies tells you nothing new.

success.narrative: Searching thoroughly, you spot a giant handkerchief, filthy and disgustingly damp, draped over a corner of the pile. You suddenly get the feeling that it is somehow important.

failure.narrative: Your thorough search turns up nothing but more revolting garbage.

secret_pocket.name: Secret Pocket
secret_pocket.description: A cramped, closet-sized space below the cave floor. The walls are the same faintly glowing canvas as the cave above, but stickier and smellier, and the air is close and damp.

exits.direction: Squeeze back up through the flap

failure.narrative: You try to squeeze back up through the flap with the giant key, but its monstrous weight defeats you; you set it down with a grunt. You are not getting anywhere with this thing — not without help.

player.description: You are a human rogue, level 4, waking with a foggy head and an empty pack: your weapons, supplies, and gear are all missing. The last thing you remember is a night out on the town.

giant_axe.name: giant battleaxe
giant_axe.description: An enormous battleaxe leaning at an angle against the cave wall. Its broad head wedges high against the canvas, its handle — about the width of a narrow sidewalk — slopes down into the murk, and its base rests amid the rubbish on the cave floor. The axe forms the only path between the upper and lower reaches of the cave.

result.narrative: It is an enormous battleaxe, its steel head pitted with age. The very tip of the blade has punched a small rip in the canvas wall.

the_rip.name: rip in the canvas
the_rip.description: A small rip in the canvas wall at the very tip of the axe blade, just within reach of someone who walks carefully out to the tip. Dull gray light winks through it.

squeeze_warned.description: The player has been warned once about squeezing through the rip.

interactions.label: Squeeze through the rip
interactions.description: Try to squeeze your whole body through the rip to the outside.

success.narrative: You get a shoulder through the rip — and hesitate. Beyond is only endless gray nothing. The GM cancels the action and asks the player if they are sure; if the player repeats the action, they go through.

failure.narrative: You twist and strain, but you simply cannot fit your body through the rip.

interactions.label: Squeeze through the rip (confirmed)
interactions.description: Despite the warning, squeeze your whole body through the rip into the gray void outside.

result.narrative: You wriggle through the rip and out into the gray. There is nothing to push against, nothing to fall toward — only the silent, endless void. You will float here forever.
The giant key tumbles through the rip and dwindles into the gray void. Without it, the padlock can never be opened. You are trapped in here forever.

success.narrative: Sticking your head through the rip, you see a gray, featureless nothing stretching in every direction — and you recognize it: the Astral Plane.

failure.narrative: Sticking your head through the rip, you see a gray, featureless nothing stretching in every direction. You have no idea what it is.

result.narrative: Looking around from the rip, you see that it is a hole in the wall of what looks like a giant sack, or bag. Above, the neck of the bag is firmly shut by a giant padlock, which dangles down to your level, its keyhole barely within reach.

success.narrative: A gray void outside; a sack impossibly vast inside. You realize what this place is: a Bag of Holding, the legendary magical storage item that exists in the prime and astral planes simultaneously.

failure.narrative: A giant sack hanging in a gray void. You cannot fathom what manner of place this is.

padlock.name: giant padlock
padlock.description: A giant padlock that firmly shuts the neck of the bag. It dangles outside the rip, hanging down to the level of the axe head, its keyhole barely within reach of someone leaning out of the rip.

hidden.description: Whether the padlock has been spotted by looking out of the rip.

interactions.label: Insert the key into the padlock
interactions.description: Push the giant iron key through the rip and into the padlock's keyhole, and try to turn it.

success.narrative: You heave the giant key through the rip and into the keyhole and, with every ounce of strength, turn it. The padlock clicks open. The neck of the bag loosens and gapes wide, and you squirm up and out — free at last! The GM narrates the escape entertainingly; the player's fate afterward (and Korbar's, if she is following) is left open-ended.

failure.narrative: You strain against the giant key, but it will not turn — and it starts to slip!

success.narrative: You manage to haul the key back in before it slips away. You can try again.

failure.narrative: The key slides from your grasp, tumbles through the rip, and dwindles into the gray void. The padlock will never open now. You are trapped forever.

interactions.label: Insert the key into the padlock (with Korbar's help)
interactions.description: With Korbar's help, push the giant iron key through the rip and into the padlock's keyhole, and try to turn it.

success.narrative: Together you and Korbar heave the giant key through the rip and into the keyhole and, with a combined effort, turn it. The padlock clicks open. The neck of the bag loosens and gapes wide, and you squirm up and out — free at last! The GM narrates the escape entertainingly; the player's fate afterward (and Korbar's, if she is following) is left open-ended.

failure.narrative: Even together, you cannot turn the giant key — and it starts to slip!

success.narrative: You manage to haul the key back in before it slips away. You can try again.

failure.narrative: The key slides from your grasp, tumbles through the rip, and dwindles into the gray void. The padlock will never open now. You are trapped forever.

webs.name: sticky webs
webs.description: Masses of sticky webbing attached to the giant axe handle, greatly dimming the glow from the canvas walls. Various strange wrapped masses of different sizes are stuck in the webs; they are too tightly wrapped to identify or extricate, and the webbing itself is too sticky to do anything useful with. The webs are much denser on the lower stretch of the handle.

result.narrative: Poking through the sticky webbing, you turn up one wrapped mass that is feebly moving: a fly, about the size of a dog, hopelessly stuck in the web.

success.narrative: Among the dense webs above, you notice eight glittering eyes watching you: a huge spider lurks in the shadows overhead.

failure.narrative: The webs here are thick and heavy, dense with tightly-wrapped masses. Nothing else catches your eye.

fly.name: Fly
fly.description: A talking fly, about the size of a dog relative to the player, hopelessly stuck in the webs on the upper stretch of the axe handle. It is mortally wounded and speaks in a weak nasally whine.

hidden.description: Whether the fly has been noticed in the webs.

delivered_warning.description: Whether the fly has spoken its warning about the spider.

attitude.description: Attitude toward the player (irrelevant; the fly is dying and its attitude never shifts).

dialogue.guidelines: The fly speaks in a weak nasally whine. It is mortally wounded and dying. It has no useful information about this place or anything else — after all, it's a fly (albeit one that talks).
dialogue.on_meeting: In a weak nasally whine, the fly warns of a giant spider that is out for blood.

spider_warning.description: The fly warns that a giant spider is out for blood.

result.narrative: From somewhere in the webs come faint groaning sounds and a weak, garbled warning.
In a weak nasally whine, the fly gasps out a warning: a giant spider is nearby, and it is out for blood.
The fly's groaning ceases. Its tiny body goes still.
Behind you, the fly's weak buzzing fades into silence. Its tiny body goes still.

spider.name: Spider
spider.description: A huge spider, hungry for blood, that lurks concealed in the webbing and shadows above the lower stretch of the axe handle. It is vain, stupid, suspicious, and malicious, but can be flattered. It can talk, though it never initiates conversation.

success.narrative: You resist the worst of the spider's venom, but it still burns.

failure.narrative: The spider's venom burns through your veins.

attitude.description: Attitude toward the player; starts hostile.

hidden.description: Whether the spider is concealed above the room.

departed.description: The spider has slunk away for good (successful persuasion).

attitude_fixed.description: Combat has been triggered; attitude is fixed at -10 permanently.

result.narrative: The spider drops from the webbing above, mandibles dripping, and lunges at you!

dialogue.guidelines: The spider is vain, stupid, suspicious, and malicious, but can be flattered; it is hungry for blood. It never initiates conversation, and grudgingly replies only if its attitude is >= -2. Being in conversation does not prevent it from attacking per its normal triggers. It knows of Korbar only as the delicious two-legs it hasn't managed to catch — yet.

flatter_spider.description: The player is pleasant or flattering toward the spider.

success.narrative: The spider preens at the flattery, its many eyes half-closing in satisfaction.

failure.narrative: The spider regards you with cold suspicion; the flattery falls flat.

provoke_spider.description: The player threatens the spider or otherwise engages negatively.

result.narrative: The spider bristles, its legs drawing tight beneath its bloated body.

persuade_passage.description: The player, while armed, tries to convince the spider to let them pass.

success.narrative: The spider sizes you up, and something about your armed readiness gives it pause. Grudgingly, it gathers its legs and slinks away into the darkness, gone for good.

failure.narrative: The spider agrees, in a silken voice, to let you pass. It is lying. It remains exactly where it is, waiting.

persuade_passage_unarmed.description: The player, while unarmed, tries to convince the spider to let them pass.

result.narrative: The spider agrees, in a silken voice, to let you pass. It is lying. It remains exactly where it is, waiting.

korbar_as_prey.description: The spider reveals it knows of Korbar only as the delicious two-legs it hasn't managed to catch — yet.

korbar.name: Korbar
korbar.description: A female Dwarf, drunk and miserable, sitting amidst the rubbish beneath a makeshift shelter made from a giant overturned soup ladle. She wears a rusty, smelly, ridiculously noisy suit of heavy plate mail, and is unarmed. Her adventuring party stuffed her in here during a stealth mission, then forgot about her. Cynical and tired, but a potential ally.

believes_spider_dead.description: Korbar has been convinced (truthfully or not) that the spider is dead.

will_fight.description: Korbar has been persuaded to stand and fight despite her fear of the spider.

following.description: Korbar is following the player as a companion.

unconscious.description: Korbar has been knocked out at 0 HP for the rest of the game (special rule replacing default death).

passive.description: Korbar cowers and takes no actions in combat. Cleared when she is persuaded to fight or aggros.

result.narrative: Korbar surges up with a roar, armor clattering, and wades in swinging her gauntleted fists!
You strike down the unconscious dwarf. It accomplishes nothing.

dialogue.guidelines: Korbar is cynical, tired, drunk, and miserable, but willing to converse. Her adventuring party stuffed her into this place during a stealth mission, then forgot about her; she is unsure how long ago — time, like space, functions strangely in here. The giant battleaxe was her weapon when she was full-sized outside. Her armor clatters every time she moves; she is unarmed. She is very afraid of spiders and refuses to fight the spider unless persuaded (see persuade_fight).
dialogue.on_meeting: A drunk, miserable dwarf woman sits amid the rubbish under an overturned soup ladle; she is astonished to see another person in here.

positive_rapport_first.description: The player engages Korbar positively — treating her respectfully, commiserating with her plight. Apply only if the player makes an actual effort (GM discretion); do not hand it out like candy. General conversation-based increases are capped at 3 total (see also positive_rapport_second/third).

result.narrative: Korbar warms slightly, a hint of gratitude breaking through her cynicism.

positive_rapport_second.description: The player continues to engage Korbar positively and makes a genuine further effort (GM discretion).

result.narrative: Korbar warms a little more, grudgingly starting to trust you.

positive_rapport_third.description: The player makes a third genuine effort to engage Korbar positively (GM discretion); this is the last general conversation-based increase.

result.narrative: Korbar gives a weary, lopsided smile. She has decided you are all right.

mock_korbar.description: The player engages Korbar negatively, e.g., makes fun of her (GM discretion).

result.narrative: Korbar's face darkens; her gauntlets clench.

convince_spider_dead.description: The player tries to convince Korbar that the spider is dead (regardless of whether it is true). The GM must judge that the player makes a convincing case based on the dialogue. If the player provides physical evidence, such as a body part cut from the spider, the CHA check is skipped (modeled as: the check is skipped if the spider is actually dead).

success.narrative: Korbar stares at you, then lets out a long, shuddering breath of relief. She believes you — the spider is dead.

failure.narrative: Korbar shakes her head grimly. She doesn't buy it — she is sure the spider is still out there.

convince_follow.description: The player convinces Korbar to follow them up (or down) the axe. She refuses to enter the secret pocket — her armor can't squeeze through the flap.

success.narrative: Korbar hauls herself up with a clatter of plate mail. 'Fine. Lead on — but slowly. This armor weighs a ton.' She will follow you, help lug the giant key, and help at the padlock.

failure.narrative: Korbar settles back under her soup ladle. She is not moving from here.

persuade_fight.description: The player persuades Korbar to stand and fight despite her fear of the spider. No check is required — the trust already earned is enough.

result.narrative: Korbar takes a deep breath and squares her shoulders, armor rattling. 'All right. If that overgrown bug shows its face, I'll fight.' From now on she fights alongside the player whenever combat breaks out in her presence.

spider_stalker.description: Korbar readily tells the player that a giant spider has been stalking her for as long as she's been here; she is very afraid of spiders and will refuse to fight it unless persuaded.

bag_of_holding_info.description: Korbar explains what this cave is — a Bag of Holding — plus basic information about what a Bag of Holding is.

secret_pocket_info.description: Korbar tells the player that a giant handkerchief on the rubbish pile covers a secret pocket in the bag floor; she hasn't been able to see inside (her armor can't fit through the flap, and she dares not remove it because of the spider).

rubbish_pile.name: pile of giant rubbish
rubbish_pile.description: A loose pile of giant-sized refuse covering the cave floor: copper pieces, empty potion bottles, used corks, lint, moldy sandwiches, and more disgusting items, heaped around the base of the giant axe.
rubbish_pile.soft_item_guidance: Nondescript giant-sized rubbish the player might pick up or use: copper pieces, empty potion bottles, used corks, lint balls, moldy sandwiches, a bent spoon, a broken belt buckle, a single moldy boot, a tattered glove, a dented tin cup, an apple core, a chipped die, a snapped quill, a length of frayed rope, a wineskin with no cork.

result.narrative: Among the refuse, you notice a giant toenail clipping, its edge curved and sharp. Pried loose, it could be wielded like a sword.

success.narrative: This is not a random pile of junk. Copper pieces, potion bottles, rations — these are some adventurer's supplies, much like your own missing pack.

failure.narrative: It's a disgusting heap of giant rubbish, nothing more.

success.narrative: An adventurer's supplies, giant-sized yet all shrunken to fit — you realize you are inside a magical Bag of Holding. The rubbish's proportions are uneven: the bag's magic shrinks items by different amounts to make them fit.

failure.narrative: The heap of giant supplies tells you nothing new.

success.narrative: Searching thoroughly, you spot a giant handkerchief, filthy and disgustingly damp, draped over a corner of the pile. You suddenly get the feeling that it is somehow important.

failure.narrative: Your thorough search turns up nothing but more revolting garbage.

toenail_sword.name: giant toenail clipping
toenail_sword.description: A giant toenail clipping, curved and sharp-edged. Gripped at the quick, it serves as a shortsword.

hidden.description: Whether the clipping has been noticed in the rubbish pile.

equip_block.note: Wielded in one hand like a shortsword: 1d6 piercing, finesse, light.

success.narrative: You work the giant toenail clipping loose from the loose pile and pull it free.

failure.narrative: The clipping is wedged in the pile and slips from your grasp.

handkerchief.name: giant handkerchief
handkerchief.description: A filthy, disgustingly damp giant handkerchief, draped over a corner of the rubbish pile. Far too large to carry.

hidden.description: Whether the handkerchief has been noticed.

moved_aside.description: The handkerchief has been lifted or shoved aside, exposing the secret flap in the floor.

interactions.label: Move the handkerchief aside
interactions.description: Lift or shove the filthy, damp handkerchief aside.

result.narrative: Gagging at the dampness, you drag the handkerchief aside. Underneath, a small flap in the canvas floor leads down into darkness.

key.name: giant iron key
key.description: A large, extremely heavy iron key. Carrying it is possible, but hauling it from place to place is arduous. It looks like it was made for an equally giant lock.

plate_armor.name: suit of plate mail
plate_armor.description: Korbar's rusty, smelly suit of heavy plate mail (AC 18), ridiculously noisy. It is far too cumbersome for the player to wear.

success.narrative: You somehow strip the armor off her. (This should be impossible while Korbar is awake.)

failure.narrative: Korbar is wearing the armor, and she is very much awake. There is no way to strip it off her.

korbar_knocked_out.description: Adventure-wide rule: Korbar falls unconscious at 0 HP instead of dying (special rule replacing default NPC death). This must be a mechanic-scope reaction, because the engine auto-clears her alive field at 0 HP, which would disable an entity-scoped reaction.

result.narrative: Korbar crumples with a crash of plate mail and lies still — unconscious, but breathing. She will not wake again.

game_over_conditions.note: The giant key has fallen through the rip (insert_key failure cascade); the padlock can never be opened.
game_over_conditions.narrative: The key is gone, swallowed by the endless gray beyond the rip. Without it, the padlock will never open. You are trapped in the bag forever.
